sections.paragraph:
μαστός, οῦ, ὁ (collateral forms μασθός GJs Bodmer [Heraclid. Miles., Fgm. 25 LCohn 1884; IG III, 238b; POslo 95, 19: 96 a.d.; PGM 7, 208; TestSol 9:4 H; JosAs 8:4 cod. A; 29:11 cod. A; Thackeray 104] and μαζός [q.v.] are found as vv.ll., but most freq. μασθός; s. Kühner-Bl. I 157; B-D-F §34, 5; W-S. §5, 27d; Mlt-H. 110) ‘one of the breasts’ (pl. in imagery Jos., Bell. 7, 189; Mel.), distinguished from the στῆθος ‘chest’; s. citation of Hom. s.v. μαζός.

① one of the mammillae, of a male, nipple (X., An. 4, 3, 6 of water ὑπὲρ τῶν μαστῶν; Eratosth. p. 33, 2; SIG 1170, 24; TestSol 9:4 LC, opp. στῆθος: a hostile spirit (δαίμων) is asked by Solomon, who has put a seal on the spirit’s chest, how it sees. Answer: ‘through my breasts’ = his nipples which function as eyes) περιεζωσμένος πρὸς τοῖς μ. ζώνην χρυσᾶν with a golden belt around his breast Rv 1:13 (μασθοῖς Tdf. in text; Diod S 1, 72, 2 περιεζωσμένοι ὑποκάτω τῶν μαστῶν).

② mammary gland or mamma of a female, breast (Hdt. et al.; Sb 6706, 9; LXX; ApcEsdr 5:2 p. 29, 26 Tdf.; Philo, Op. M. 38; Mel., P. 52, 384) Lk 11:27; 23:29; GJs 5:2; 6:3; 19:2; ApcPt Fgm. 2 p.12, 25.—DELG. M-M.

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

Heraclid Heraclid , perieg., III–II b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

IG IG = Inscriptiones Graecae—List 3

POslo POslo = Papyri Osloenses—List 4

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

H H = Hermas; cited, unless otherwise noted, according to MWhittaker; II a.d.—List 1

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Thackeray Thackeray = HT., A Grammar of the OT in Greek according to the Septuagint—List 6

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

vv.ll. vv.ll. = variae lectiones (variant readings)

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

W-S. W-S. = GWiner, Grammatik des neutestamentlichen Sprachidioms, ed. PSchmiedel—List 6

Mlt-H. Mlt-H. = JMoulton/WHoward, A Grammar of NT Greek II: Accidence and Word-formation—List 6

  1. pl. = plural

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Eratosth Eratosth , III b.c.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

opp. opp. = opposed to, opposite

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

ApcEsdr ApcEsdr = Apocalypse of Esdras—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

ApcPt ApcPt = Apocalypse of Peter—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 621
